title: asplenium ceterach subsp. mediterraneum
intro: Asplenium ceterach subsp. mediterraneum is a subspecies of a fern plant known as the Mediterranean asplenium or wall-rue, which is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Asplenium ceterach subsp. mediterraneum include treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and certain skin conditions, as well as providing relief from fever and rheumatism. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are made by drying and processing the leaves and rhizomes of the plant.
